body:
]> 24/07/2026 7:33 A little fresh snow and a big night of snowmaking!.

We had another 2cm of fresh snow in the early evening yesterday, alongside another solid night of snowmaking. 201 guns fired up overnight, with some from as early as 2pm yesterday afternoon, and they're still running this morning.

It's a cold start to the day, with the temperature in Perisher Valley currently -3.7 degrees, and a westerly wind blowing around 26kph. The forecast is calling for a partly cloudy day with a top of 1 degrees and the chance of some snow flurries, however the sky is clear this morning and the mountains look stunning. Winds will stay westerly ranging from 25 to 40 km/h in the valley and stronger up top, so rug up to keep warm!

There are 34 groomed runs across the resort with the snow surface likely to be firm and fast again with a nice layer of man-made on top. There are 29 lifts scheduled to spin, there's a chance wind may impact some lift operations today, so as always keep an eye on the lift status report and Perisher App for all the latest updates and real-time lift status.

It's the final day of the ACT and Regional NSW Interschools, with racing continuing today on Front Valley and Leichhardt. Congratulations to all of the competitors who have participated this week.

We also have the Three Peaks Demo on again, set up at the bottom of Mt P6, so if you're in the market for some new gear or just want to try out the latest tech go and check it out! The Red Bull Boom Box will also be out there bringing the vibes. In other live music, check out Will Clift at Mid Station, DJ Bolta at Blue Cow Bistro and Matty Rogers Band in Jax.

If you feel like som retail therapy, we have some great deals in Perisher Sports, Slopestyle and Snowdome at the moment, freshen up your kit by purchasing 1 item of outerwear and scoring 50% off a second! Or head into store to discover more savings.

Looking ahead, Christmas in July will bring some festive magic to Perisher tomorrow, it's one not to miss! Check the Events page or follow Perisher's socials to stay across all the action.

]]> weather-blank Kosciuszko Road: There are icy stretches from Dainers Gap to Perisher.

Guthega Road: Guthega Road: Snow on the road from The Link Road to the Village.

]]> 69.3 2 4 29 201 34 *Natural snow depth is supplemented with snowmaking in certain areas. Natural snow depth is measured at roughly 1,830m by Snowy Hydro Limited who have invested in new instrumentation across the Snowy Scheme and can now provide more frequent snow depth readings at a site close to the Spencers Creek snow course. This report reflects conditions at the date and time of this report. Conditions, lifts status and operations subsequent to this report may differ.
